source_evidence: Section 4.4, Eq. (14): phi_F = (pi/2)(N_phi - 2)/N_phi, for a domain discretized with constant spacing on phi. 'Making phi_F constant ensures that the refinement is approximating the same boundary value problem, and thus a smooth convergence to the analytical closed-form solution.'
purpose: Computes the latitude of the applied moment ring when a spherical/orthogonal domain is discretized with constant angular spacing in phi; used to control whether refinement approximates the same boundary value problem.
assumptions: Domain discretized with constant spacing on the phi angle
If phi_F varies with discretization, each mesh approximates a different problem and convergence is not guaranteed
Fixing the first and last phi elements (and evenly distributing the rest) makes phi_F constant across discretizations
The optimal volume V_opt increases with phi_F (Eq. 12), so growing phi_F under refinement causes divergence
formula_or_algorithm: For constant spacing in phi: phi_F = (pi/2) * (N_phi - 2) / N_phi. To hold phi_F constant instead, fix the size of the first and last phi elements and evenly distribute the remaining elements.
validation_opportunities: convergence test: constant phi_F converges to V_opt while increasing phi_F diverges (Fig. 15, Tables 6-7)
limit check as N_phi grows (phi_F -> pi/2)

reuse_risk.explanation: Simple angular relation; the important insight is procedural (hold phi_F fixed to preserve the boundary value problem), not computationally complex.

missing_information: Exact scheme for fixing first/last elements to hold phi_F constant
